title: Loki’s Chains and the Island’s Fear
description: On pages 4–6, this part follows Unknown person #4, Loki, Unknown person #5, Usopp, Unknown person #6, King Harald as God’s Knights and Elbaf’s ruling tensions push the island toward open conflict while the Straw Hats and giants react to the threat.
scene: God’s Knights and Elbaf’s ruling tensions push the island toward open conflict while the Straw Hats and giants react to the threat.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: He was a rare ancient giant...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...enhanced with even more power and an undying body!!!"

dialogue.speaker: Loki
dialogue.text: Those traits made him a true monster...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: ...and yet he was still killed?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Who could have??? is something unforeseen lurking on Elbaf?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We should have the Abyss Pentagon carved by Harald inspected._

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: That shall not be necessary.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It would only result in the death of another Knight...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Every Covenant comes with a price to be paid, even for Mu.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Huff... huff...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He had no Qualms about lowering his head to appease humans.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: All he ever sought was communion with other nations and peoples.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: HARAD was a total Aberration.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And yet, he was born with the blood of those savage, battle-loving giants.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: had we admitted him into the government flippantly, his very presence might have destroyed it from within.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Elbaf should never have known a King of his disposition.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Forging a Covenant With a Cooperative, Ancient Giant Like Him...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: ...was an unprece- dental opportunity.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: This disappointing outcome...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...proves that history does not lie. We were always going to be incompatible.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: never forget...

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Elbaf is of the "D"!!!

dialogue.speaker: Loki
dialogue.text: Loki... After this...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: The country will be in disarray.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: I'm heading out to sea, old-timer.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: I'll take the surviving soldiers with me.

dialogue.speaker: King Harald
dialogue.text: What about Harald's final request???

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Tell the people of Elbaf...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: ...what happened here!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: There's no way I can tell them the truth!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Just drop it, all of you!

summary: This part advances God’s Knights Tighten Their Grip through 31 cleaned dialogue beats, focusing on the immediate conflict, character reactions, and the larger Elbaf mystery.
